steps.title: Factor the polynomial
steps.content: Start by factoring out the greatest common factor: $$2x^3+38x^2+168x=2x(x^2+19x+84).$$ The quadratic factors because $7\cdot12=84$ and $7+12=19$: $$2x(x^2+19x+84)=2x(x+7)(x+12).$$

steps.title: Convert roots to factors
steps.content: The negative roots $-7$ and $-12$ correspond to factors $x+7$ and $x+12$. Therefore the possible positive values of $b$ are $7$ and $12$. The smallest possible value is $7$.
